title: Trichocephalus spicatus
taxonomy: Trichocephalus spicatus is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically belonging to the division Magnoliophyta and class Magnoliopsida. It further categorizes into the order Lamiales, family Lamiaceae, and genus Trichocephalus. The species is then identified as Trichocephalus spicatus. More specifically, the plant is considered to be an ornamental herb with spiky leaves, and its classification is a part of ongoing scientific research. Further studies are needed to accurately determine its classification.
uses: Trichocephalus spicatus is primarily a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa and Asia. Its medicinal uses include treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant is also used to treat respiratory issues like coughs and colds, as well as digestive problems like diarrhea. Its leaves and roots are often used to prepare herbal remedies, which are believed to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Trichocephalus spicatus.

parts: Trichocephalus spicatus, also known as the spicate beard lichen, is not typically recognized as a plant species. However, the spicate beard lichen is often studied in the context of lichens. Unfortunately, I do not have information on the medicinal parts of the spicate beard lichen.
preparations: Trichocephalus spicatus, a type of fern, is not well-documented in terms of herbal preparations.
